title: ctenitis thwaitesii
intro: Ctenitis thwaitesii, also known as Thwaites' ctenitis, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, with properties that include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as exhibiting potential antidiabetic and antimicrobial properties. Ctenitis thwaitesii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
